← → keys | F = fullscreen
Data Science Project

Rakuten France
Multimodal Product Classification

Business case, data, and the full modelling pipeline
Team project — Sandeep Grover · Jonathan Vints · Thomas Maisch
Public benchmark: Rakuten France Multimodal Product Data Classification
ENS Challenge Data (challenge 35) · also on Kaggle · Amoualian et al., ECIR 2021

The business case

The problem
  • Rakuten = global marketplace, millions of sellers
  • Every product needs the right catalogue category
  • Wrong category → invisible in search & recommendations
Why it is hard at scale
  • Free-form titles in many languages
  • A third of products have no description
  • Manual sorting of 84,916 listings is slow & error-prone
The solution
  • Predict the category automatically
  • From the product's own text and image

What the listings actually look like

Books (used)
Olivia: Personalisiertes Notizbuch / 150 Seiten / Punktraster / Ca Din
🌐 German — not French∅ no description
Magazines
Journal Des Arts (Le) N° 133 Du 28/09/2001 - L'art Et Son Marche Salon
∅ no description
Gaming accessories
Grand Stylet Ergonomique Bleu Gamepad Nintendo Wii U - Speedlink Pilot
</> HTML in text
“ergonomique pour GamePad Nintendo Wii U.<br> Pour un confort optimal et une précision maxi…”
Toys
Peluche Donald - Europe - Disneyland 2000 (Marionnette À Doigt)
∅ no description
Books
La Guerre Des Tuques
French · has description
“Luc a des id&eacute;es de grandeur. Il veut organiser un jeu de guerre de b…”
Stationery
Conquérant Sept Cahier Couverture Polypro 240 X 320 Mm 96 Pages 90g Se
French · has description
“CONQUERANT CLASSIQUE Cahier 240 x 320 mm seyès incolorecouverture en Polypr…”
Three problems in one glance: multiple languages · HTML inside descriptions · often no description at all

Imbalance · chi-square goodness-of-fit

Imbalance is real
  • Biggest 9,814 vs smallest 693 — a 14.2× gap
  • Chi-square goodness-of-fit: χ² = 35,282, p < 0.001
  • Classes are significantly non-uniform — not chance
  • Potential solution: report weighted-F1 (challenge metric) + macro-F1 for rare classes; class weighting; stratified split
Ref: Amoualian et al. (2021), ECIR — Rakuten dataset

Missing data · MNAR

Missingness is informative
  • Missing rate varies strongly by category
  • Chi-square independence: χ² = 42,953, V = 0.72, p < 0.001
  • So absence depends on the class — this is MNAR, not random (MCAR/MAR)
  • Potential solution: add a desc_missing flag — never drop the rows
Ref: Little & Rubin (2019), Statistical Analysis with Missing Data

Text length · Kruskal-Wallis

Length differs by class
  • Median ~301 chars, but heavily skewed with a long tail
  • Kruskal-Wallis (non-parametric ANOVA): H = 35,118, p < 0.001
  • Length varies significantly across categories (skewed → no normal ANOVA)
  • Potential solution: keep designation_len / description_len
Ref: Kruskal & Wallis (1952), JASA 47(260):583-621

Languages · Wilson 95% CI

Real language strata
  • Mostly French (~81%; ~71% on titles alone), plus English (~10%), German and a European tail
  • Wilson 95% CI stays valid for the rare languages (Wald breaks near 0)
  • Confirms English/German are genuine sub-populations, not noise
  • Potential solution: a French / multilingual language model — CamemBERT (French RoBERTa), FlauBERT or XLM-R
Ref: Wilson (1927), JASA 22(158):209-212

Text preprocessing — clean, then split

Step 1 · per listing, before the split
Clean the text
  • Merge title + description → one field, lowercase (keep French accents)
  • Strip HTML (BeautifulSoup), URLs, e-mails, numbers
  • Tokenize (TweetTokenizer) · remove FR/EN/DE stopwords (nltk) · Snowball French stemming
  • Flag missing descriptions
→
Step 2
Split 80 / 20
  • 80% train · 20% test
  • Stratified — both keep the 27-class balance
  • Test held out — used only to evaluate
Cleaning is per-listing, so it is safe on the whole dataset before the split. TF-IDF (next) learns corpus-wide statistics, so it is fit only after the split, on the training set only, then applied (transform) to the test set.
Ref: Richardson, Beautiful Soup (HTML parser, crummy.com); Amoualian et al. (2021), ECIR — Rakuten dataset

Word frequency: before vs after stopword removal

Frequent ≠ useful
  • Left (raw): most frequent = stopwords (de, la, et…) → removed by nltk FR/EN/DE
  • Right (after removal): top uni/bi-grams = content words (cm, couleur, piscine, eau…) — the TF-IDF features
  • Rarest terms (one-off typos/serials) dropped by min_df = 3 + 20,000 cap
Ref: Sparck Jones (1972), J. Documentation 28(1)

Step 3 — TF-IDF: the feature matrix

How each score is built
  • Uses bigrams too (word-pairs): "jeux video"
  • TF: how often a word appears in a listing
  • IDF: how rare it is across all listings
  • Score = TF × IDF: rare words win, filler → ~0
Worked example (real IDF from our data)
  • "nintendo" in 825 of 83,502 docs → IDF = 5.6 → high
  • "couleur" in 18,030 docs → IDF = 2.5 → low (common content word)
  • Stopwords (de, le, et) removed before TF-IDF — never columns
Ref: Sparck Jones (1972), J. Documentation 28(1); Pedregosa et al. (2011), scikit-learn
The matrix structure
  • Rows = listings · Columns = 20,000 terms
  • Each cell = a TF-IDF score · ~99% zero (sparse)
  • Fit TF-IDF on TRAIN only → then transform (apply) to test — test unseen at fit (no leakage)
nintendoswitchjeuxcouleur…
listing 15.65.000…
listing 2003.72.5…
listing 30000…
………………
rows = listings  ×  columns = 20,000 terms · ~99% zeros

EDA summary — findings & potential recommendations

FindingKey numberPotential recommendation
Class imbalance14.2× gap · χ² = 35,282 · p<0.001report weighted-F1 (challenge metric) + macro-F1 for rare classes; class weighting; stratified split
Missing descriptions35.5% · MNAR (χ² = 42,953, p<0.001)add desc_missing flag · never drop the rows
HTML in descriptionsin most non-empty descriptionsstrip with BeautifulSoup before TF-IDF
Text length by classKruskal-Wallis · p < 0.001add designation_len / description_len
Duplicate listings1,414 exact dups (1.7%) → removeddropped exact duplicates → 83,502 rows; 2,651 dup-title (3.1%) kept
Multilingual contentFR ~81% · EN ~10% · DE ~2% (langid + Wilson CI)French / multilingual model — CamemBERT, XLM-R
Text → featuresmerged text · TF-IDF · 20,000 · 80/20vectorise the merged title+description; fit on train only
Outcome — the TF-IDF feature matrix (handed to the modelling stage)
  • Rows: 83,502 listings — 80/20 split → ~66,800 train / ~16,700 test
  • Columns: 20,000 TF-IDF terms + desc_missing + length features
  • Feature type: numeric TF-IDF scores — sparse (~99% zeros)
  • Target: 27 product categories

Text modelling - classical baselines

Same cleaned text, classical pipeline
  • Corpus filtered to French + English (~90% of listings)
  • Merge title + description · strip HTML and e-mails · tokenize · FR+EN stopwords removed
Features & class balance
  • TF-IDF fit on train only, compressed with truncated SVD (1,000 components)
  • SMOTE balances the training classes
Models & result
  • KNN and XGBoost tuned with 5-iteration BayesSearchCV
  • KNN weighted-F1 0.72 · XGBoost weighted-F1 0.76

Text modelling - XLM-RoBERTa (0.87)

Why XLM-RoBERTa
  • Multilingual RoBERTa (100 languages) fits the ~81% French corpus with its EN/DE tail - no language routing
  • MAX_LEN 256: ~79% of texts fit under it; 512 mostly adds padding
Fine-tune (FREEZE_LAYERS = 0)
  • Embeddings frozen, all 12 encoder layers trainable; head = dropout 0.3 + Linear 768 → 27
  • AdamW lr 2e-5 · batch 16 · grad-clip 1.0 · up to 10 epochs · ReduceLROnPlateau · early stopping · best checkpoint restored
Cleaning is the lever
  • Light cleaning (BeautifulSoup + URL/email only) = 0.87 · heavy cleaning = 0.82
  • Stopwords KEPT: RoBERTa was pretrained on running text, so over-cleaning removes context
Result
  • Weighted-F1 0.87 vs challenge text baseline 0.8113
  • Class weights, designation/description as a sentence pair, and a linear-decay schedule also reached 0.87 - but the hardest classes improved with class weights

Text model - training dynamics

F1 and loss disagree on when to stop
  • F1 (left): val 0.815 → 0.880, peaking ~epoch 8; train climbs to 0.961
  • Loss (right): val loss bottoms ~epoch 4 then rises while F1 is still improving
  • Optimising F1 instead of loss gives 0.88 vs 0.87 - the model gets better at the right class but more confident in its mistakes

Text model - confusion (XLM-RoBERTa, F1 0.87)

Where the text model still confuses
  • 22% of Books (2705) predicted as used Books (10) - new vs used, indistinguishable from text alone
  • Same confusable families as the image branch: Trading figures 1180 · Childcare/baby 1320 · Board games 1281
  • These are exactly the classes the image branch later helps resolve

Image preprocessing - corner crop

Crop to the product
  • Most photos sit in a large white border
  • Average of the 4 corner pixels = background reference
  • Pixels darker than 85% of it = product
  • Crop the bounding box → resize 224×224 → ImageNet normalization

Backbone choice - EfficientNet-B0

Small but strong
  • 5.3M params vs ResNet50 25.6M
  • ImageNet top-1 77.1% vs 74.9%
  • Efficiency from depthwise-separable MBConv blocks + squeeze-and-excitation

Tuning the image model (0.6515)

Full fine-tune wins
  • ~25-combination ablation (freezing / augmentation / weights / sampler / classifier) on 10k → best 0.5482
  • Freezing HURTS: deep ImageNet layers must adapt to product imagery
  • Final: full fine-tune, new head 1280 → 512 → 27, Adam 1e-4, up to 20 epochs, early stopping patience 3 on val loss
  • Validation F1 plateaus ~0.65 while train F1 keeps rising - early stopping intercepts
  • RESULT: weighted-F1 0.6515 vs ResNet50 baseline 0.5534

Where the image model fails

Two confusable families
  • Errors concentrate in two visually similar families
  • Cluster A: Figurines · Trading figures · Toys · Board games · Model making
  • Cluster B: Books (used) · Magazines · Books-mags lots · Books
  • Exactly where text disambiguates

What the CNN looks at - GradCAM

Looking where it matters
  • Activation maps of the last conv layer, weighted by the gradient of the predicted class score
  • Book (Books used 10) → heat sits on the title band, i.e. the network reads the cover text
  • Drone carrying case (Model making 1300) → heat sits on the case body, not the white background

Multimodal fusion (0.8984)

Two frozen encoders, one small head
  • Frozen EfficientNet-B0 (1280-d) + frozen XLM-RoBERTa (768-d) → concat 2048-d
  • Trainable head: Linear 512 / ReLU / dropout → 27 classes (1,062,939 params = 0.4%)
  • Head lr 2e-5 - improves slowly, reaching its maximum at epoch 9
  • Frozen-feature comparison (5 seeds): image-only 0.6787 · text-only 0.8761 · fused 0.8985
  • Per-class gains largest for Books / Magazines / Imported games - where the photo disambiguates

Fusion - training dynamics

Improves gradually - best at epoch 9
  • Only the head trains (0.4% of parameters); it improves slowly, reaching its maximum val F1 0.8984 at epoch 9
  • Val F1 rises to its maximum 0.8984 by epoch 9 while train F1 climbs to ~0.96
  • Both frozen encoders are already converged - the head only learns how to weigh them

Fusion - confusion matrix

Clean diagonal across 27 classes
  • Each row is labelled by category name and prdtypecode
  • The families that defeated the image branch (figurines/toys/games; books/magazines) are now resolved by text
  • Residual error concentrates where both modalities are weak

Where the image actually helps

Per-class contribution of the image branch
  • Biggest gains: Books 2705, Books used 10, Imported games 40, Books/mags lots 2403
  • These are exactly the classes where the cover or packaging carries what the text lacks
  • A few text-strong classes (Wall decor 2060, Toys 1280) are marginally diluted
  • Mean over 5 seeds - the gain is +0.0224, consistent across seeds

Where we landed — public leaderboard

Submitted to the public Rakuten France Multimodal Product Data Classification benchmark as Sandeep_Jonathan_Thomas_Liora (16 Jul 2026): public score 0.8902, rank #15 out of the all-time board.
